message:
My 8-year-old spotted our go-bag in the closet last week and asked, 'Mom, why do we have a backpack for emergencies?' I started explaining—you know, the whole calm-and-ready thing—and she goes, 'So we're *prepared*, not scared.' 

Kids are honestly smarter sometimes. She totally got it.

Since then, she's been the one reminding me to check the water supply and asking if our important documents are in a safe spot. Turns out when you frame preparedness as 'being ready' instead of 'what if something bad happens,' the whole family leans in. Even the little ones.

Does anyone else have a kid or family member who's surprisingly into this stuff? I'm genuinely curious what clicks for them—this refresher on family plans gave us some good talking points at dinner.
